summary: Added the project’s first Doxygen configuration and a GitHub Pages publishing workflow so generated SSBM decomp documentation is rebuilt and deployed on every push to master. The Doxyfile targets C-oriented source documentation, scans src and docs recursively, emits HTML under build/doxygen, uses Doxygen Awesome styling checked out during CI, and defines decomp-specific aliases for offsets, member sizes, original names, and glossary links.
what_changed: Created a top-level Doxyfile for the project named "SSBM Decomp" with output directed to build/doxygen.
Configured Doxygen to optimize for C output, extract all entities, recurse through INPUT = src docs, and generate HTML.
Limited documented file patterns to project-relevant source/documentation inputs: *.c, *.h, *.dox, and *.md.
Added custom ALIASES for decomp documentation metadata: offset/at, size/sz, sizebyte/sz1, sizebits/szb, sizebit/szb1, was/origname, and glossary/g helpers.
Configured HTML styling through Doxygen Awesome CSS files: doxygen-awesome.css and doxygen-awesome-sidebar-only.css.
Added .github/workflows/publish.yml to run on pushes to master, install doxygen and graphviz, generate docs, create .nojekyll, upload the HTML artifact, and deploy to GitHub Pages.
The workflow checks out jothepro/doxygen-awesome-css into tools/doxygen-awesome-css instead of requiring the styling assets to live permanently in the repository.
smart_moves: Kept large Doxygen Awesome assets out of the repository by having the GitHub Action clone the CSS repository at build time, matching reviewer guidance that it should be an optional dependency.
Used GitHub Pages’ modern deployment permissions and actions/upload-pages-artifact plus actions/deploy-pages, making docs publishing automatic after master commits.
Added .nojekyll so GitHub Pages will serve generated Doxygen files with underscores correctly.
Encoded common decomp documentation needs as aliases, especially offsets, bit ranges, byte/bit sizes, original names, and glossary links, reducing repeated hand-written markup in future comments.
Set FILE_PATTERNS narrowly to C, headers, Doxygen pages, and Markdown instead of leaving Doxygen’s broad default language list, reducing irrelevant parsing.
decomp_lessons: For repository-wide documentation tooling, separate default generated configuration from meaningful project edits when possible; reviewer feedback noted that Doxyfile changes are easier to review if custom changes are isolated from the stock Doxyfile.
Decomp projects benefit from standardized doc macros for structure offsets, bitfields, member sizes, and original symbol names because those facts recur throughout headers and source comments.
Documentation styling dependencies with bulky binary artifacts should be fetched by CI rather than vendored when local builds can still fall back to default styling.

When enabling auto-published docs, keep the workflow reproducible: install explicit documentation tools, generate into a build directory, upload only the generated HTML, and deploy through GitHub Pages artifacts.
Doxygen config formatting matters for reviewability; aligned space indentation in multi-line values such as ALIASES and FILE_PATTERNS makes GitHub diffs easier to read.
naming_conventions: Project documentation title uses PROJECT_NAME = "SSBM Decomp".
Custom doc aliases use short and long forms: offset with at shorthand, size with sz shorthand, sizebits with szb shorthand, and glossary with g shorthand.
Original symbol/name metadata is represented with the was{1} alias, producing an "Original Name" xref item.
Offset and size documentation is grouped into Doxygen xref lists named "Member Offsets", "Member Sizes", and "Original Names".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code-matching changes were made in this PR.

The reusable decomp tactic is documentation-oriented: record layout facts such as offsets, bit positions, and sizes through Doxygen aliases so future struct/header work can expose matching-relevant data consistently.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Requested that the GitHub Action clone doxygen-awesome-css instead of embedding it in the tree because some PNG artifacts were large, and noted it made sense as an optional dependency if local builds could use default styling.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether the only top-level Doxyfile changes were ALIASES helpers and suggested separating custom edits from adding the default Doxyfile if there were more changes.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Flagged mismatched indentation in Doxyfile FILE_PATTERNS and said ALIASES also needed space indentation to align in GitHub diff view.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Approved with: "Looks great! Lots of useful stuff in the published docs".

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add Doxygen comments using @offset, @size, @was, or @glossary aliases.
Check whether later workflow changes pin the Doxygen Awesome checkout or action versions more tightly.
Look for subsequent documentation PRs that reorganize docs/ pages or add glossary entries.
Search for any later Doxyfile changes to warning policy, source browsing, graph generation, or input exclusions.
